sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("Wall start at key rows:")
for row in [1, 16, 24, 36, 44, 52, 63]:
    line = ascii_lines[row]
    for j, c in enumerate(line):
        if c == 'G':
            print(f"  Row {row}: wall at col {j}")
            break

print("\nFigure position:")
for row in range(52, 58):
    line = ascii_lines[row]
    for j, c in enumerate(line):
        if c == 'c':
            print(f"  Row {row}: figure at col {j}")
            break

print("\nRow 24 slice:")
print(f"  {ascii_lines[24][:60]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see! Clicking the blue block at rows 24-27 moved the wall at the TOP:
- Wall at row 1 moved from col 52 to col 48 (4 cols LEFT)
- Wall at row 16 moved from col 52 to col 48 (4 cols LEFT)
- Wall at row 24 moved from col 4 to col 8 (4 cols RIGHT)
- Figure stayed at col 18 (unchanged)
So this blue block affects the top part of the wall, not the bottom part where the figure is.
Let me summarize what I've learned:
- Blue at rows 44-47 (lower): moves wall RIGHT at bottom (figure moves right)
- Blue at rows 36-39 (middle-lower): moves wall LEFT at bottom (figure moves left)
- Blue at rows 24-27 (middle-upper): moves wall LEFT at top (doesn't affect figure)
- Blue at rows 16-19 (upper): unknown effect yet
So I have two controls for the bottom wall: lower blue (right) and middle-lower blue (left). The figure is at col 18, gap at col 28. I need the figure to move 10 more columns right.
But the lower blue stopped working after 4 clicks. Let me check if I can click it more after clicking other blue blocks. Maybe the other blue blocks "reset" the limit.
Actually wait, let me re-examine. After clicking the middle-lower blue (which moved the wall left), the lower blue might work again. Let me try clicking the lower blue again.
But first, let me understand the state better. The current state is:
- Figure at col 18, gap at col 28
- Wall at row 52 starts at col 20
- Wall at row 1 starts at col 48 (was col 52 initially)
I clicked:
1. Lower blue × 4 (figure moved 10→22)
2. Middle-lower blue × 1 (figure moved 22→18)
3. Middle-upper blue × 1 (top wall moved, figure unchanged)
So the figure is at col 18 now. I need it at col 28. That's 10 more columns right.
Let me try clicking the lower blue again to see if it works after clicking other blue blocks.
